_comment: Risk Factor Registry. Loaded at runtime by app/risk/config.py -- NOT hardcoded in Python
(Phase 4 brief SS2/SS5). Override the path with RISK_FACTORS_PATH.
ADDING A FACTOR REQUIRES NO CODE CHANGE. Append an object to `factors`; the engine picks
it up by matching `trigger_condition` against incoming RiskSignals. The only constraint is
that some signal collector must emit a signal whose `signal_type` matches.
CALIBRATION HONESTY: these are EXPERT-SET weights, not statistically fitted ones.
docs/phase-0-dataset-audit.md SS14 established this dataset cannot support calibration
(only 60 of 2,000 clients carry behavioural ground truth; client names resolve to nothing
authoritative). Presenting them as fitted would be a lie. They encode a compliance posture:
a confirmed sanctions hit alone should reach CRITICAL; structural/jurisdictional factors
Contribution is then capped at `max_contribution` and the total capped at 100.
metadata_equals  -- every key/value must match signal.metadata

_repeated_signal_excluded_event_types_no: REPEATED_SIGNAL means 'the same KIND of finding recurred' (e.g. 3 adverse-media articles),
which is corroboration. OTHER is a catch-all bucket shared by unrelated factors, so counting
it produced a real false alert in testing ('2 new OTHER findings' = high-risk sector +
ownership opacity, which are two different facts, not a repetition). Excluded here rather
than hardcoded in app/risk/alerts.py, so the policy stays configurable.

factors.name: High-confidence sanctions match
factors.description: Entity resolution returned a HIGH_CONFIDENCE match against a sanctions/watchlist entity.

factors.name: Possible sanctions match
factors.description: Entity resolution returned a POSSIBLE match needing human corroboration.

factors.name: Entity resolution conflict
factors.description: A candidate matched on name but contradicted on a corroborating attribute. Recorded for transparency; contributes nothing to the score.

factors.name: Adverse media hit
factors.description: An adverse-media provider returned an article mentioning the entity.

factors.name: Ownership exposure to a flagged party
factors.description: An ownership-graph traversal linked the subject to a resolved sanctions entity.

factors.name: Politically exposed person
factors.description: Client master carries an upstream PEP label. Requires enhanced due diligence; not itself an adverse finding.

factors.name: Upstream sanctions flag
factors.description: Client master carries a pre-existing sanctions_flag from an upstream system. NOT a match this system found -- see docs/phase-0-dataset-audit.md SS3.

factors.name: High-risk geography
factors.description: Client domiciled in a FATF-listed or OFAC-sanctioned jurisdiction.

factors.name: High-risk sector
factors.description: Client operates in a sector classified High risk.

factors.name: Ownership opacity
factors.description: Client carries a non-zero ownership opacity score.

factors.name: Transaction typology flag
factors.description: Pre-computed AML typology flags on the client's transactions (structuring, rapid movement, OFAC corridor, trade mispricing).

factors.name: Provider unavailable
factors.description: A provider errored/timed out, so this monitoring cycle's coverage was incomplete. Weight 0: incomplete coverage is a transparency fact, never grounds to raise a client's risk.
